pilots.name: Environment Setup Verification
pilots.description: Verify that le-wm, dm-control, mujoco, peft and necessary-compositionality all import correctly. Run a single forward pass of LeWM (encoder + predictor) on a random 64x64 RGB frame. Verify MuJoCo Walker environment with custom gravity, friction, and body density can be instantiated.
script_outline: import lewm; import dm_control; import mujoco; import peft; print('all imports OK')
Run lewm forward pass on random tensor (1, 3, 64, 64)
Instantiate Walker with gravity=4.9 (0.5g) and verify observation shape
Instantiate Walker with friction=2.0x and collect 5 steps
pilots.pass_criteria: All imports succeed; forward pass returns latent of expected shape; Walker variants run without error
pilots.decision: If any import fails: document missing package and install before proceeding. This is a blocking gate.

pilots.name: P1: Framework Validation — Friction-Only Holdout
pilots.description: Train LeWM-SIGReg on friction levels {0.5x, 2.0x} in Walker (gravity=1.0g, mass=1.0x fixed). Use exactly 100 trajectories per training friction level. Then probe with linear Ridge regression (sklearn) on: (a) friction=1.0x (interpolation holdout, 100 trajectories), (b) friction=3.0x (extrapolation holdout, 100 trajectories). Targets: friction factor label (classification → R²), joint angles (regression). Seed=42, 5 training epochs (for speed), batch_size=32.
script_outline: Collect 100 trajectories for friction in {0.5x, 1.0x, 2.0x, 3.0x} with Walker (gravity=1.0g, mass=1.0x)
Train LeWM-SIGReg on friction {0.5x, 2.0x} trajectories for 5 epochs, seed=42
Extract frozen encoder embeddings for all 4 friction levels
Train Ridge probe (alpha=1e-3) to predict friction label; compute R² on {1.0x, 3.0x}
Train Ridge probe to predict joint angles; compute R² on {1.0x, 3.0x}
pilots.pass_criteria: Training loss converges in 5 epochs; R² in-distribution (friction {0.5x, 2.0x}) > 0.3 for at least one target; framework runs end-to-end without error

decision_rules.aggressive_holdout: R² drops < 5% relative on friction=1.0x → extend to extrapolation holdout or 3-factor combinations
decision_rules.reduce_factor_range: R² drops > 40% relative on interpolation → reduce gravity/friction range to narrower intervals
decision_rules.flag_as_trivial: R² < 0.1 in-distribution → revisit training budget (more epochs needed before probing)

pilots.name: P2: LoRA Feasibility on ViT-Tiny Predictor
pilots.description: Using the checkpoint from P1, apply LoRA with rank=4 to the predictor Q/V matrices only (using peft library). Fine-tune on 50 trajectories from friction=1.0x (interpolation holdout). Measure R² recovery on probe for friction label and joint angles. Run for 20 gradient steps (fast feasibility check, not convergence).
script_outline: Fine-tune for 20 steps on 50 friction=1.0x trajectories
Extract updated embeddings; re-run linear probe; compute R² recovery
pilots.pass_criteria: LoRA inserts without error; fine-tuning converges (loss decreases from step 1 to 20); R² recovery metric is computable

decision_rules.lora_viable: R² recovery > 50% → proceed with full LoRA sweep (ranks {2, 4, 8, 16}, data sizes {10, 25, 50, 100, 200})
decision_rules.increase_rank: R² recovery < 20% → extend rank range to {32, 64} in full study; report low-rank limitation as negative result
decision_rules.flag_architecture_issue: LoRA insertion fails or causes error → investigate ViT-Tiny architecture compatibility; fallback to full fine-tuning as ceiling

pilots.name: P3: Principal Angle Analysis Verification
pilots.description: Using P1 checkpoint embeddings, run the necessary-compositionality principal angle analysis on 3 factor subspaces. Verify the tool runs end-to-end on the le-wm embedding space (ViT-Tiny produces 192-dim latents).
pilots.pass_criteria: Principal angle matrix (3x3) computed; mean cosine similarity in valid range [0, 1]; no import errors or numerical issues

pilots.name: P4: IIS Computation Feasibility
pilots.description: Using P1 checkpoint, compute IIS for the gravity-friction concept pair on 100 trajectories. Verify that: (1) linear probe direction can be extracted; (2) Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization works; (3) latent shift and predictor rollout are numerically stable; (4) random-direction IIS baseline is near 0.5.
pilots.pass_criteria: IIS(gravity, friction) computed; random baseline IIS in [0.4, 0.6]; no NaN or overflow in rollout

pilots.name: P5: Physical Coupling Estimation Feasibility
pilots.description: Using pilot trajectory data (100 trajectories per combo), estimate coupling strength c(friction, gravity) via finite-difference mixed partial derivative of velocity variance. Verify numerical stability with 100 samples.
pilots.pass_criteria: Coupling strength computed; non-negative; finite; output JSON has correct schema

pilot_execution_order.note: Must pass before any other pilot
Gate for full study design; results inform holdout aggressiveness
Determines LoRA rank range for full study; can run in parallel with P3-P5 after P1
